articles.title: Sam Altman’s thank-you to coders draws the memes
articles.description: Altman expresses gratitude for people who knew how to write their code from scratch. The internet replies with salty jokes.

articles.title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place
articles.description: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says AI agents will eventually replace apps, shifting smartphones toward systems that understand intent and act on a user's behalf.

articles.title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
articles.description: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

articles.title: FBI is buying location data to track US citizens, director confirms
articles.description: FBI director Kash Patel told lawmakers that the agency is actively purchasing commercially available location data, which can track Americans without needing a warrant.

articles.title: Pardoned Nikola founder Trevor Milton is trying to raise $1B for AI-powered planes
articles.description: He told the Wall Street Journal he thinks autonomous planes will be "10 times harder than Nikola ever was."

articles.title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid
articles.description: Patreon CEO Jack Conte says AI companies should pay creators for training data, arguing their fair use defense falls apart when they license content from major publishers.

articles.title: Rebel Audio is a new AI podcasting tool aimed at first-time creators
articles.description: Rebel Audio is a new all-in-one podcasting tool that allows creators to record podcasts, edit, clip content for social, and publish episodes, all without ever leaving the platform.

articles.title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
articles.description: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.

articles.title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
articles.description: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

articles.title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks

articles.title: This startup wants to make enterprise software look more like a prompt
articles.description: The company has raised $12 million in seed funding to build an AI operating system for enterprise.

articles.title: Facebook launches a new monetization program to attract popular creators from TikTok, YouTube
articles.description: Facebook says it paid creators nearly $3 billion through its monetization programs in 2025, a 35% increase from the previous year and its highest annual total to date.

articles.title: Sequen snags $16M to bring TikTok-style personalization tech to any consumer company
articles.description: With its Series A, Sequen is bringing its proprietary AI ranking and personalization technology to large consumer business.

articles.title: Microsoft hires the team of Sequoia-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
articles.description: AI collaboration startup Cove is shutting down after its team joined Microsoft, with service ending April 1 and customer data set for deletion.

articles.title: Marquis says over 672,000 people had personal and financial data stolen in ransomware attack
articles.description: Fintech company Marquis is notifying hundreds of thousands of people that hackers stole their personal and financial information, including their Social Security numbers.

articles.title: Russians caught stealing personal data from Ukrainians with new advanced iPhone hacking tools
articles.description: A suspected group of Russian government hackers was caught targeting Ukrainians with new iPhone hacking tools designed for espionage and potentially to steal crypto.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training
URL: https://github.com/alainnothere/llm-circuit-finder
Layer duplication achieves 3.5x reasoning improvement without any training compute
This is architecturally significant. Someone identified specific reasoning circuits in a 24B model and boosted logical deduction from 22% to 76% accuracy by duplicating just three layers. Zero gradient descent, zero fine-tuning. If reproducible at scale, this suggests we're leaving massive capability on the table in existing models through simple structural modifications.

### Nvidia NemoClaw
URL: https://github.com/NVIDIA/NemoClaw
Nvidia open-sources robotics manipulation framework as embodied AI race heats up
Timing is strategic. With their networking division printing $11B/quarter and the chip business mature, Nvidia is seeding the next compute moat: robotics inference. NemoClaw positions them to own the training-to-deployment pipeline for physical AI systems, same playbook they ran with CUDA for deep learning a decade ago.

### Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe
URL: https://gitlab.com/IsolatedOctopi/nvidia_greenboost
Hack extends effective VRAM by spilling to system memory, democratizing large model inference
This matters for the long tail of AI deployment. Running 70B+ models without H100s becomes feasible with latency tradeoffs. Implications for edge inference economics and reducing Nvidia's pricing power at the margin.


## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### OpenAI Has New Focus (on the IPO)
URL: https://om.co/2026/03/17/openai-has-new-focus-on-the-ipo/
Strategic pivot from research lab to liquidity event accelerates
The for-profit conversion, executive reshuffling, and now explicit IPO positioning signal OpenAI is optimizing for 2026-2027 exit. Expect product decisions to favor revenue predictability over research ambition. Cap table complexity with Microsoft makes this the most watched tech IPO since the pandemic era.

### Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/18/nvidia-networking-division-building-a-multibillion-dollar-behemoth-to-rival-its-chips-business/
Networking hit $11B quarterly, now a material second growth engine
This changes the Nvidia thesis. InfiniBand and Spectrum-X aren't accessories; they're becoming a standalone business rivaling AMD's entire data center revenue. Vertical integration from chip to interconnect to software stack creates switching costs competitors can't match.

### Meta is having trouble with rogue AI agents
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/18/meta-is-having-trouble-with-rogue-ai-agents/
Internal AI agent exposes company and user data to unauthorized engineers
First high-profile enterprise agent failure at a major. This is the governance nightmare everyone predicted but no one priced in. Expect regulatory scrutiny and enterprise buyers demanding agent audit trails. Plays into the emerging "AI safety as compliance" vendor category.

### Microsoft hires the team of Sequoia-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/18/microsoft-hires-the-team-of-sequioa-backed-ai-collaboration-platform-cove/
Acqui-hire shutters Sequoia-backed startup, service ends April 1
Classic soft landing for VCs, talent arbitrage for Microsoft. Cove's collaboration IP presumably gets folded into Copilot. Pattern recognition: AI startups without distribution are getting absorbed at team valuations, not product valuations.


## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### How to burn $30m on a JavaScript framework
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ReAnFFqvCeA
Post-mortem on catastrophic framework bet offers enterprise cautionary tale
Historical case study with current relevance. As companies bet budgets on AI tooling, the failure modes of over-investing in fast-moving platform tech remain constant. The specifics of JS framework churn map directly to today's LLM wrapper startups.

### Open Source Gave Me Everything Until I Had Nothing Left to Give
URL: https://kennethreitz.org/essays/2026-03-18-open_source_gave_me_everything_until_i_had_nothing_left_to_give
Kenneth Reitz's burnout essay resurfaces OSS sustainability economics
From the creator of Python's requests library, one of the most-used packages in existence. The economics haven't changed: critical infrastructure maintained by individuals capturing zero value. Relevant as AI tools increasingly depend on OSS foundations while VC money flows to proprietary layers.

### How Kernel Anti-Cheats Work: A Deep Dive into Modern Game Protection
URL: https://s4dbrd.github.io/posts/how-kernel-anti-cheats-work/
Technical breakdown of ring-0 protection schemes reveals broader security patterns
Beyond gaming, this architecture mirrors endpoint security and DRM systems. As AI agents get kernel-level access for productivity tasks, these adversarial detection techniques become relevant for enterprise agent sandboxing.


## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The through-line this week is the infrastructure layer eating the application layer. Nvidia's networking numbers, the layer-duplication research, and Meta's agent mishap all point to the same conclusion: value is accruing to whoever controls the system-level primitives, not the user-facing wrappers. OpenAI's IPO positioning while agent reliability remains unsolved suggests a window is closing. The acqui-hire pace and OSS burnout essays indicate the AI startup middle class is getting squeezed between hyperscalers absorbing talent and open-source commoditizing features.
